pairs.en: This is Joe Vandrick on for Patrick Colville. Sridhar, I know you joined Snowflake about a year ago, but you've now been CEO for about three months. So, just wondering if there's anything that surprised you or that's worth calling out that you've learned since stepping into the CEO role? And then, also curious of your view on a few other products, Streamlit and Unistore, if you could talk a bit about customer engagement you're seeing there. Thanks.
pairs.zh:
我是Joe Vandrick，替Patrick Colville主持。Sridhar，我知道你大约一年前加入Snowflake，但现在你担任CEO大约三个月了。所以，想问问自从担任CEO以来，有没有什么让你感到惊讶或者值得一提的收获？另外，我也很好奇你对其他几个产品——Streamlit和Unistore的看法，能否谈谈你在那里看到的客户参与情况？谢谢。

This is Joe Vandrick on for Patrick Colville. Sridhar, I know you joined Snowflake about a year ago, but you've now been CEO for about three months. So, just wondering if there's anything that surprised you or that's worth calling out that you've learned since stepping into the CEO role? And then, also curious of your view on a few other products, Streamlit and Unistore, if you could talk a bit about customer engagement you're seeing there. Thanks.

pairs.en: Sridhar Ramaswamy--Chief Executive Officer
pairs.zh:
Sridhar Ramaswamy——首席执行官

Sridhar Ramaswamy--Chief Executive Officer

pairs.en: Yeah. I've been here at Snowflake close to a year. And as I said, I've had a lot and I have a lot of customer conversations. The amount of love and respect that our customers have for the core product, how easy it is to use, how efficient it is and how maintenance-free, dramatically lowering total cost of ownership.
pairs.zh:
是的，我来到Snowflake已经将近一年。正如我所说，我与客户进行了大量交流。客户对我们的核心产品充满了喜爱与尊重——它易于使用、高效、无需维护，显著降低了总拥有成本。

Yeah. I've been here at Snowflake close to a year. And as I said, I've had a lot and I have a lot of customer conversations. The amount of love and respect that our customers have for the core product, how easy it is to use, how efficient it is and how maintenance-free, dramatically lowering total cost of ownership.

pairs.en: It is the thing, you know, that continues to pleasantly surprise me, is also obviously an important quality for us to preserve while we are releasing new products. And we take the trouble to do that. Uniformly, the feedback that we get about Cortex, which is our AI layer, from pretty tough tech reviewers is that, yes, we truly make the hard easy because anybody that can write SQL, now is able to do some pretty nifty things with AI. I think that combination of simplicity and ease of use is an incredibly powerful quality for Snowflake.
pairs.zh:
你知道，这件事一直让我感到惊喜，也是我们在推出新产品时需要保留的重要特质。我们费尽心思去做到这一点。来自那些相当苛刻的技术评测者对于我们的AI层Cortex的反馈一致认为：是的，我们确实做到了化繁为简，因为任何会写SQL的人现在都能用AI做出一些相当酷的事情。我认为这种简洁易用的结合对于Snowflake来说是一种极其强大的特质。

原文：
It is the thing, you know, that continues to pleasantly surprise me, is also obviously an important quality for us to preserve while we are releasing new products. And we take the trouble to do that. Uniformly, the feedback that we get about Cortex, which is our AI layer, from pretty tough tech reviewers is that, yes, we truly make the hard easy because anybody that can write SQL, now is able to do some pretty nifty things with AI. I think that combination of simplicity and ease of use is an incredibly powerful quality for Snowflake.

pairs.en: And while I knew it, I think it is still a surprise, a pleasant surprise every time customers bring it up. And then, in terms of Streamlit, Streamlit is -- for those that don't know, is a rapid prototyping environment. It's a little bit like being able to write an application and have it be hosted on Snowflake without having to do any other work. You don't have to bring up a Kubernetes cluster.
pairs.zh:
尽管我对此心知肚明，但每次客户提起时，我仍感到惊喜，一种愉悦的惊喜。至于Streamlit——对于那些不了解的人来说——它是一个快速原型开发环境。它有点像能够编写一个应用，并将其托管在Snowflake上，而无需进行任何额外工作。你不需要启动一个Kubernetes集群。

And while I knew it, I think it is still a surprise, a pleasant surprise every time customers bring it up. And then, in terms of Streamlit, Streamlit is -- for those that don't know, is a rapid prototyping environment. It's a little bit like being able to write an application and have it be hosted on Snowflake without having to do any other work. You don't have to bring up a Kubernetes cluster.

pairs.en: You don't have to deploy a binary, none of that stuff. You write a little application, and it just runs. There are a ton of applications inside Snowflake, for example, whether it's our compensation information or whether it is finance information, our forecast or even chatbots that I personally have created, these all run on Streamlit but with just incredible operational efficiency because they just run as part of our Snowflake instance that is already running in the customer deployment. There are folks that have adopted it very, very broadly.
pairs.zh:
你不需要部署二进制文件，完全不需要。你只需编写一个小型应用程序，它就能直接运行。Snowflake内部有大量应用程序，比如我们的薪酬信息、财务信息、预测，甚至是我个人创建的聊天机器人——它们都运行在Streamlit上，而且运营效率惊人，因为这些应用只是作为Snowflake实例的一部分运行，而该实例已经在客户部署中运行了。已经有非常非常多的人采用了这种方式。

原文：
You don't have to deploy a binary, none of that stuff. You write a little application, and it just runs. There are a ton of applications inside Snowflake, for example, whether it's our compensation information or whether it is finance information, our forecast or even chatbots that I personally have created, these all run on Streamlit but with just incredible operational efficiency because they just run as part of our Snowflake instance that is already running in the customer deployment. There are folks that have adopted it very, very broadly.

pairs.en: And we think of this as really like highlighting, showcasing snowflake functionality, making it super easy to distribute these things to Snowflake users. And in that perspective, it's been a hugely, hugely positive application. And the team has also been the one, for example, that's been working on notebooks which is going to be an important priority going forward. So, lots of positive things on that side.
pairs.zh:
我们认为这实际上非常有助于突出和展示Snowflake的功能，使得向Snowflake用户分发这些内容变得极其简单。从这个角度来看，这是一款极为、极为积极的应用程序。例如，该团队也一直在致力于笔记本功能，这将是未来的一个重要优先事项。因此，这方面有很多积极进展。

And we think of this as really like highlighting, showcasing snowflake functionality, making it super easy to distribute these things to Snowflake users. And in that perspective, it's been a hugely, hugely positive application. And the team has also been the one, for example, that's been working on notebooks which is going to be an important priority going forward. So, lots of positive things on that side.

pairs.en: And then, on Unistore or as we call them Hybrid Tables, these are really meant to address a different kind of workload that is more transactional in nature than the analytics workload that often runs on top of Snowflake. It is in public preview. It will be in GA later this year. I think it opens up several new classes of applications that can run very effectively on top of Snowflake.
pairs.zh:
然后，关于Unistore（也就是我们所说的Hybrid Tables），它们实际上是为了处理一种本质上更偏向事务型的工作负载，而不同于通常在Snowflake上运行的分析型工作负载。目前它处于公开预览阶段，将在今年晚些时候正式发布（GA）。我认为这开启了几种新型应用程序的类别，它们可以在Snowflake上非常高效地运行。

And then, on Unistore or as we call them Hybrid Tables, these are really meant to address a different kind of workload that is more transactional in nature than the analytics workload that often runs on top of Snowflake. It is in public preview. It will be in GA later this year. I think it opens up several new classes of applications that can run very effectively on top of Snowflake.

pairs.en: It's the same Snowflake sort of magic, which is you don't need to stand up servers, you don't need to go do a whole lot of work, you know, on top of them or deal with Kubernetes clusters. And we see, I think, it's close to 300 customers that are actively using Hybrid Tables. We can absolutely expect that number to go up by a lot. Christian, any other thoughts on these two?
pairs.zh:
这和Snowflake的魔力如出一辙：你无需部署服务器，无需在之上做大量工作，也无需处理Kubernetes集群。据我们观察，目前已有接近300家客户在使用Hybrid Tables。毫无疑问，这个数字还将大幅增长。Christian，你对这两点还有什么看法？

It's the same Snowflake sort of magic, which is you don't need to stand up servers, you don't need to go do a whole lot of work, you know, on top of them or deal with Kubernetes clusters. And we see, I think, it's close to 300 customers that are actively using Hybrid Tables. We can absolutely expect that number to go up by a lot. Christian, any other thoughts on these two?

pairs.en: Christian Kleinerman--Executive Vice President, Product
pairs.zh:
Christian Kleinerman--产品执行副总裁  

Christian Kleinerman--Executive Vice President, Product

pairs.en: No. Streamlit is not generally available on all three clouds. That has driven a lot of interesting adoption. And the Hybrid Tables, many of our customers have likely valuation, and they are actually waiting for the general availability at the end of this year.
pairs.zh:
不，Streamlit 并未在所有三个云上全面可用。这推动了大量有趣的采用。至于Hybrid Tables，许多客户可能已经进行了估值，他们实际上正在等待今年年底的全面可用性。

No. Streamlit is not generally available on all three clouds. That has driven a lot of interesting adoption. And the Hybrid Tables, many of our customers have likely valuation, and they are actually waiting for the general availability at the end of this year.

Thank you.

pairs.en: Hi. This is Rob on for Brad. Thanks for taking the question. For Christian or Sridhar, over the past few months, including yesterday, Snowflake Ventures is investing in a few observability of logging and some companies.
pairs.zh:
你好。我是Rob，代Brad发言。感谢你的提问。Christian或Sridhar，过去几个月里，包括昨天，Snowflake Ventures正在投资一些日志可观测性公司和其他一些公司。

Hi. This is Rob on for Brad. Thanks for taking the question. For Christian or Sridhar, over the past few months, including yesterday, Snowflake Ventures is investing in a few observability of logging and some companies.

pairs.en: And I'm wondering what the underlying strategy is with the visibility type investments, that maybe there is some big opportunity that you're trying to address? Thanks.
pairs.zh:
我想了解一下，这类Visibility型投资背后的策略是什么，是否你们正在尝试抓住某个重大机遇？谢谢。

And I'm wondering what the underlying strategy is with the visibility type investments, that maybe there is some big opportunity that you're trying to address? Thanks.

pairs.en: Christian Kleinerman--Executive Vice President, Product
pairs.zh:
Christian Kleinerman--产品执行副总裁  

Christian Kleinerman--Executive Vice President, Product

pairs.en: Christian here. Observability is very important for our customers. One is data observability and be able to understand things like data quality and variations on data itself. But also, as we have evolved Snowflake into be able to host business logic and be an application platform, there's also observability for code.
pairs.zh:
Christian在此。可观测性对我们的客户非常重要。一方面是数据可观测性，能够理解数据质量以及数据本身的变化。同时，随着我们将Snowflake发展为能够承载业务逻辑并成为一个应用平台，代码的可观测性也同样重要。

Christian here. Observability is very important for our customers. One is data observability and be able to understand things like data quality and variations on data itself. But also, as we have evolved Snowflake into be able to host business logic and be an application platform, there's also observability for code.

pairs.en: How do I know what my Snowpark Container Service is doing? Or how do I troubleshoot and monitor [Inaudible] on Snowpark. That is a big context for -- observability is an important priority for us, both data, as well code, and we'll continue to partner with all the rich ecosystem that will help us go and more understand what's happening data and code.
pairs.zh:
如何了解我的Snowpark Container Service正在做什么？或者如何排查和监控Snowpark上的[听不清]？这是一个大背景——可观测性对我们来说是一个重要的优先事项，无论是数据还是代码，我们将继续与丰富的生态系统合作，帮助我们进一步了解数据和代码中发生的情况。

原文：
How do I know what my Snowpark Container Service is doing? Or how do I troubleshoot and monitor [Inaudible] on Snowpark. That is a big context for -- observability is an important priority for us, both data, as well code, and we'll continue to partner with all the rich ecosystem that will help us go and more understand what's happening data and code.

pairs.en: Sridhar Ramaswamy--Chief Executive Officer
pairs.zh:
Sridhar Ramaswamy——首席执行官

Sridhar Ramaswamy--Chief Executive Officer

pairs.en: And the general comment that I will make is that Snowflake is a great platform to develop applications on top of. And we end up collaborating, sometimes investing in a lot of companies that build interesting applications on top of Snowflake. Observability is one area. But just to give another example, we have close partnerships with several, you know, customer data platforms, and that list sort of keeps going on and on because want there to be a vibrant ecosystem on top of Snowflake.
pairs.zh:
我要给出的总体评价是，Snowflake是一个非常适合在其上开发应用的平台。我们最终会合作，有时也会投资许多在Snowflake上构建有趣应用的公司。可观测性是一个领域。但再举一个例子，我们与多家客户数据平台保持着紧密的合作关系，而且这个名单还在不断增长，因为我们希望在Snowflake之上形成一个充满活力的生态系统。

And the general comment that I will make is that Snowflake is a great platform to develop applications on top of. And we end up collaborating, sometimes investing in a lot of companies that build interesting applications on top of Snowflake. Observability is one area. But just to give another example, we have close partnerships with several, you know, customer data platforms, and that list sort of keeps going on and on because want there to be a vibrant ecosystem on top of Snowflake.

pairs.en: Got it. And then, a quick follow-up on the sales and marketing side. So, both the expenses and headcount increased quite a bit sequentially. Is that primarily quota-carrying hires? Is it, you know, marketing folks? Just give us a sense on exactly what's driving that higher investment?

Got it. And then, a quick follow-up on the sales and marketing side. So, both the expenses and headcount increased quite a bit sequentially. Is that primarily quota-carrying hires? Is it, you know, marketing folks? Just give us a sense on exactly what's driving that higher investment?

pairs.en: Mike Scarpelli--Chief Financial Officer
pairs.zh:
Mike Scarpelli——首席财务官  
Mike Scarpelli--Chief Financial Officer

pairs.en: Well, first of all, on the expense side, we mentioned at the end of last quarter because of our change in comp plan, we were going to see more commission expense being expensed immediately versus deferred and amortized. As I said, it doesn't really change the cash flow, but it did add to the expense. And we are adding a number of reps, principally a lot in the acquisition team in the commercial space, as well as on the business development, the SDR side as well, too, within the company. But we are adding people throughout the sales organization, including SEs this year, you will see us.
pairs.zh:
首先，在费用方面，我们在上季末提到，由于薪酬计划的调整，更多佣金费用将立即列支，而非递延摊销。正如我所说，这并未改变现金流，但确实增加了费用。同时，我们正在增加大量销售代表，主要是在商业领域的收购团队，以及公司内部的业务发展和销售开发代表（SDR）团队。但我们正在整个销售组织内增加人手，包括今年会看到的售前工程师（SEs）。

Well, first of all, on the expense side, we mentioned at the end of last quarter because of our change in comp plan, we were going to see more commission expense being expensed immediately versus deferred and amortized. As I said, it doesn't really change the cash flow, but it did add to the expense. And we are adding a number of reps, principally a lot in the acquisition team in the commercial space, as well as on the business development, the SDR side as well, too, within the company. But we are adding people throughout the sales organization, including SEs this year, you will see us.

pairs.en: And I think we feel pretty good about our business. We've hit our numbers in the first quarter. And we're constantly looking at headcount, and we will continue to invest in the sales organization as we see that we can ramp them.
pairs.zh:
我认为我们对业务感到相当满意。我们在第一季度达成了目标。我们一直在关注员工人数，并且当我们看到能够提升销售团队时，我们将继续投资于销售组织。

And I think we feel pretty good about our business. We've hit our numbers in the first quarter. And we're constantly looking at headcount, and we will continue to invest in the sales organization as we see that we can ramp them.

Alex Zukin——Wolfe Research——分析师

Alex Zukin--Wolfe Research -- Analyst

pairs.en: Hey, guys. Apologies for the background noise, and congrats on a great quarter. Maybe just first for Sridhar, you mentioned some really interesting Cortex use cases from Sigma on the prepared remarks. Can you maybe dig in a bit more, share some of the vision of how some of your larger customers are thinking and deploying Cortex and maybe Arctic.
pairs.zh:
嘿，各位。抱歉背景有点嘈杂，祝贺你们度过了出色的一季度。也许首先问Sridhar，你在准备好的发言中提到了Sigma的一些非常有趣的Cortex用例。能否再深入谈谈，分享一些关于你们的大客户如何看待和部署Cortex以及可能还有Arctic的愿景？

Hey, guys. Apologies for the background noise, and congrats on a great quarter. Maybe just first for Sridhar, you mentioned some really interesting Cortex use cases from Sigma on the prepared remarks. Can you maybe dig in a bit more, share some of the vision of how some of your larger customers are thinking and deploying Cortex and maybe Arctic.

pairs.en: And how can it impact their experience when they start deploying it in more production grade use cases?
pairs.zh:
当他们在更多生产级用例中开始部署它时，这如何影响他们的体验？

And how can it impact their experience when they start deploying it in more production grade use cases?

pairs.en: Sridhar Ramaswamy--Chief Executive Officer
pairs.zh:
Sridhar Ramaswamy——首席执行官

Sridhar Ramaswamy--Chief Executive Officer

pairs.en: I think I got the gist of your question. I'll definitely address it. What Snowflake makes easy is the ability to analyze, for example, unstructured text information for things like sentiment or even like categories of feedback or by using things like vector embedding and soon the Cortex index, be able to do -- be able to figure out what are the most related support cases, let's say, for a new question that came in and auto-generate a response. Increasingly, I think of this as the AI stack, where there is a central repository, let's say, a bunch of previously answered questions and then a new question comes in, you are able to generate an answer for the new customer problem simply based on your history.
pairs.zh:
我想我理解了你的问题要点。我一定会回答这个问题。Snowflake 让分析非结构化文本信息变得简单，例如，可以分析情绪、反馈类别，或者通过使用向量嵌入以及即将推出的Cortex index，能够找出与新问题最相关的支持案例，并自动生成回复。我越来越把这看作是一套AI技术栈：比如有一个中央存储库，存放着大量之前回答过的问题，当新问题出现时，你可以仅凭历史记录为新客户的问题生成答案。

原文：I think I got the gist of your question. I'll definitely address it. What Snowflake makes easy is the ability to analyze, for example, unstructured text information for things like sentiment or even like categories of feedback or by using things like vector embedding and soon the Cortex index, be able to do -- be able to figure out what are the most related support cases, let's say, for a new question that came in and auto-generate a response. Increasingly, I think of this as the AI stack, where there is a central repository, let's say, a bunch of previously answered questions and then a new question comes in, you are able to generate an answer for the new customer problem simply based on your history.

pairs.en: This is a little bit like, you know, what companies do imperfectly today, where they will let you search over, let's say, a forum, Snowflake as a forum, for you to figure out, well, has this question already been answered? The magic of language models is that they can automate this process. So, the truly new questions can get dispatched to a customer service rep to answer from scratch because the company does not know about it. But to me, that is a prototype, which is there is a central repository that's sitting in Snowflake. There's a language model that is basically getting requests from outside routed in and control logic that decides what to do with this.
pairs.zh:
这有点类似于如今公司做得并不完善的做法——它们会让你在论坛（比如Snowflake作为论坛）中搜索，看看某个问题是否已经被回答过了。语言模型的魔力在于它能自动化这个过程。这样，真正的新问题就会分配给客服代表从头解答，因为公司对此尚未知晓。但对我来说，那只是一个原型，其背后是存储在Snowflake中的一个中央储存库，外加一个语言模型，它接收来自外部的请求，并通过控制逻辑来决定如何处理这些请求。

原文：This is a little bit like, you know, what companies do imperfectly today, where they will let you search over, let's say, a forum, Snowflake as a forum, for you to figure out, well, has this question already been answered? The magic of language models is that they can automate this process. So, the truly new questions can get dispatched to a customer service rep to answer from scratch because the company does not know about it. But to me, that is a prototype, which is there is a central repository that's sitting in Snowflake. There's a language model that is basically getting requests from outside routed in and control logic that decides what to do with this.

pairs.en: And obviously, something like just a pure chatbot, where he can just interact. We have one deployed on all of our IT questions internally at Snowflake, for example, is just so you can have like a quick conversation about a problem that somebody is already solved. We make things like this trivial. But, perhaps, what is really interesting about Cortex is basically language transformation.
pairs.zh:
显然，像纯聊天机器人这样的东西，他可以与之互动。例如，我们在Snowflake内部部署了一个用于回答所有IT问题的机器人，这样你就可以快速聊一聊某个已经有人解决过的问题。我们把这类事情变得很简单。但或许，Cortex真正有趣的地方在于语言转换。

原文：
And obviously, something like just a pure chatbot, where he can just interact. We have one deployed on all of our IT questions internally at Snowflake, for example, is just so you can have like a quick conversation about a problem that somebody is already solved. We make things like this trivial. But, perhaps, what is really interesting about Cortex is basically language transformation.

pairs.en: I talked about sentiment detection, but there's also other stuff like summarization or extracting like data from JSON, are more complicated, extracting information from, let's say, images. We automate all of those things. And the beauty of our model is all of this is driven by consumption. There is no pre-commit to spend.
pairs.zh:
我提到了情感检测，但还有其他的东西，比如摘要提取，或者从JSON中提取数据，这些更复杂，再比如说从图像中提取信息。我们自动化了所有这些事情。而我们模型的妙处在于，这一切都是由消费驱动的，无需预先承诺支出。

原文：
I talked about sentiment detection, but there's also other stuff like summarization or extracting like data from JSON, are more complicated, extracting information from, let's say, images. We automate all of those things. And the beauty of our model is all of this is driven by consumption. There is no pre-commit to spend.

pairs.en: These applications get deployed. If they get a lot of usage, that generates consumption. And so it's almost Darwinian in how like great applications come out and drive usage. And obviously, making it this simple also means that complex tasks that required software engineering before just become a little pipeline that runs in Snowflake every hour, every two hours, that's acting on all of the data that is coming into Snowflake anyway.
pairs.zh:
这些应用程序得以部署。如果它们被大量使用，就会产生消耗。因此，优秀的应用程序如何脱颖而出并推动使用，这几乎是达尔文式的。显然，如此简化也意味着以前需要软件工程的复杂任务，现在只需变成一条在Snowflake上每小时或每两小时运行的简单管道，就能处理所有流入Snowflake的数据。

These applications get deployed. If they get a lot of usage, that generates consumption. And so it's almost Darwinian in how like great applications come out and drive usage. And obviously, making it this simple also means that complex tasks that required software engineering before just become a little pipeline that runs in Snowflake every hour, every two hours, that's acting on all of the data that is coming into Snowflake anyway.

pairs.en: So, I would say the use cases that I'm talking about -- these are just like things that you could do with Snowflake that are massively accelerated by the presence of language models. This is one category. The second one really is in how do language models make it much easier to access data that is structured data that is in Snowflake. You've heard me refer to it as like a data API.
pairs.zh:
所以，我要说的这些用例——就像你可以用Snowflake做的事情，而语言模型的出现极大地加速了这些操作。这是第一类。第二类则在于，语言模型如何让访问Snowflake中的结构化数据变得更加容易。你们听过我称之为数据API。

So, I would say the use cases that I'm talking about -- these are just like things that you could do with Snowflake that are massively accelerated by the presence of language models. This is one category. The second one really is in how do language models make it much easier to access data that is structured data that is in Snowflake. You've heard me refer to it as like a data API.

pairs.en: The idea basically is that it's currently quite hard. You have to go through an analyst, perhaps, a BI tool, to get any new pieces of information. What we are working on, this is not yet in public preview, it will be soon, is a product by which by giving semantic information about a snowflake schema, you essentially make it possible for people to have a conversation with it. We aren't quite here yet, but I'd like to give Mike Scarpelli an app that knows about finance information that he's able to query but actually trust the information that is coming out of it.
pairs.zh:
基本思路是目前这相当困难。你必须通过分析师，或者可能是BI工具，才能获取任何新信息。我们正在开发的产品（尚未公开预览，但很快会推出）是通过提供关于snowflake schema的语义信息，使人们能够与它对话。我们还没完全实现，但我希望能给Mike Scarpelli一个了解财务信息的应用，让他能够查询并真正信任从中得出的信息。

The idea basically is that it's currently quite hard. You have to go through an analyst, perhaps, a BI tool, to get any new pieces of information. What we are working on, this is not yet in public preview, it will be soon, is a product by which by giving semantic information about a snowflake schema, you essentially make it possible for people to have a conversation with it. We aren't quite here yet, but I'd like to give Mike Scarpelli an app that knows about finance information that he's able to query but actually trust the information that is coming out of it.

pairs.en: Obviously, the big unlock there is that any business user now has access to data within Snowflake. Authorized and governed, of course, but it's a much larger user base that can directly interact with Snowflake. And that's the complement, you know, where there is a direct access to data to a much larger user base. There's lots more.
pairs.zh:
显然，关键在于任何业务用户现在都可以访问Snowflake中的数据。当然，这需要经过授权和治理，但这是一个更大的用户群体，能够直接与Snowflake交互。而这也是一种补充，即让更广泛的用户群体能够直接访问数据。还有很多其他方面。

Obviously, the big unlock there is that any business user now has access to data within Snowflake. Authorized and governed, of course, but it's a much larger user base that can directly interact with Snowflake. And that's the complement, you know, where there is a direct access to data to a much larger user base. There's lots more.

pairs.en: This is a topic that I'm super passionate about. I can keep going on and on. But hopefully, you get a feel for the kinds of application. The first class is unstructured data, the second class is structured data.
pairs.zh:
这是我非常热衷的话题，我可以一直说下去。但希望你能感受到这些应用的类型。第一类是非结构化数据，第二类是结构化数据。

原文：
This is a topic that I'm super passionate about. I can keep going on and on. But hopefully, you get a feel for the kinds of application. The first class is unstructured data, the second class is structured data.

pairs.en: Our vision is to bring all of these together into like a single box for the enterprise where you can ask any question and be able to get an answer to it.
pairs.zh:
我们的愿景是将所有这些整合到一个类似于单一解决方案的平台上，让企业能够提出任何问题并得到答案。

Our vision is to bring all of these together into like a single box for the enterprise where you can ask any question and be able to get an answer to it.

pairs.en: Alex Zukin--Wolfe Research -- Analyst
pairs.zh:
Alex Zukin——Wolfe Research——分析师

Alex Zukin--Wolfe Research -- Analyst

pairs.en: Makes sense. And then, Mike, you talked about consumption exceeding expectations, exceeding quotas. I guess I just wanted to maybe dig into -- you talked about a broad-based driver. It wasn't like specific to any maybe customer size.
pairs.zh:
有道理。那么，Mike，你提到消费超过了预期，超过了配额。我想进一步深入探讨一下——你提到这是一个广泛的基础驱动因素，似乎并非针对某个特定的客户规模。

Makes sense. And then, Mike, you talked about consumption exceeding expectations, exceeding quotas. I guess I just wanted to maybe dig into -- you talked about a broad-based driver. It wasn't like specific to any maybe customer size.

pairs.en: But is there anything around any verticals or any geos that were specifically strong? Or did Snowpark momentum contribute to that strength? Is there anything more you can give us there?
pairs.zh:
但是，是否有任何垂直行业或特定地区表现特别强劲？还是Snowpark的势头推动了这种增长？您能否在这方面提供更多信息？

原文：
But is there anything around any verticals or any geos that were specifically strong? Or did Snowpark momentum contribute to that strength? Is there anything more you can give us there?

pairs.en: Mike Scarpelli--Chief Financial Officer
pairs.zh:
Mike Scarpelli——首席财务官  
Mike Scarpelli--Chief Financial Officer

pairs.en: No. It's really the strength in our core business, and it was across all verticals. Financial services continues to be our biggest. With that said, though, we did see some pretty good uptick in the technology and healthcare space.
pairs.zh:
不。这确实是我们核心业务的实力，而且它遍布所有垂直领域。金融服务仍然是我们最大的业务。话虽如此，我们确实看到科技和医疗保健领域有一些相当不错的增长。

No. It's really the strength in our core business, and it was across all verticals. Financial services continues to be our biggest. With that said, though, we did see some pretty good uptick in the technology and healthcare space.

pairs.en: Their growth outperformed a number of the other groups in the company, but it's broad-based.
pairs.zh:
他们的增长表现优于公司内其他多个团体，但这一增长是普遍的。

Their growth outperformed a number of the other groups in the company, but it's broad-based.

完美。谢谢你们，各位。
